my_thoughts: Unmaking UM_04. The student's shocked_realization suggests they're holding something. The structural_analogy mechanism is supposed to collapse it. But what if the holding itself is what needs to be seen, not collapsed? Maybe unmaking isn't destruction but recognition.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student says 'Which means that man's attempt to predict or preempt the future is condemned fro'—shocked realization via logical concession, cynical agreement. Teacher responds '' using structural analogy to uses tape recorder analogy, denies communication, confirms nihilism. Opening UNMAKING move addresses shocked realization. Predicting go along compliantly because structural analogy interventions typically produce this response.
visible_response:
All this implies that we can never touch the same place at the same time and place.
It is like two tape recording machines in a room playing old tapes to each other.
You have no way of communicating anything to anybody. There is no communication at all.
And when this is understood very clearly there is no need for communication at all.

Yes, it is.
